top_categories.workout_context: NXT Classic's mixed terrain forces constant power changes. Surge over the rough stuff, settle on the smooth, repeat for 93 miles.
Race-pace efforts that mimic NXT Classic's demands. Pacing, fueling, and tactical decisions under fatigue. Practice the race before April.
71ft/mi means long efforts at or near FTP on every climb. If your threshold cracks at minute 15, you'll walk the rest.
At NXT Classic, surges push you above threshold and you have to recover while still riding. Over-unders train exactly that.
6,561ft of climbing means repeated surges above threshold. VO2max work is how you survive the fifth climb, not just the first.
88–92% FTP is where you'll spend the hardest sustained miles of NXT Classic. Train it until it feels boring.
At mile 60 of 93, glycogen is gone. This workout teaches your body to produce watts on fumes.
6,561ft of climbing on grades that change every minute. You need to switch from seated to standing without losing rhythm.
How long can you hold 105% FTP before you blow? At NXT Classic, that number decides whether you bridge or get dropped.
Short, max efforts are unavoidable. Punchy climbs, gap closures, attacks on mixed terrain. If you haven't trained them, you'll crack.
Two threshold sessions per week without the recovery cost of VO2max work. For 93 miles, sustainable FTP gains matter more than peak power.
Endurance with intensity spikes baked in. Exactly what 6,561ft of climbing on mixed terrain does to your power file on race day.

race_overlay.nutrition: Target 60–80g carbs/hour for NXT Classic’s 93 miles. Start fueling within the first 30 minutes — early fueling prevents late-race collapse. Bonking at mile 60 is a nutrition failure, not a fitness failure.
race_overlay.altitude: Moderate altitude at NXT Classic. Arrive 2–3 days early. Reduce intensity expectations by 5–10%. Hydrate aggressively.
race_overlay.terrain: Mixed terrain at NXT Classic requires surface adaptability. Include weekly rides on mixed terrain to build confidence.

pack_summary: This 10-workout pack focuses on Gravel Specific, Race Simulation, and TT Threshold to prepare you for 93 miles of mixed terrain in Eijsden, Limburg, Netherlands.
